forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KRRE4PJV0N1GSE92MF45GGPV
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8880'
+project: plugin-arch
+title: 'plugin: CliServer stdio subprocess transport'
+---
+## What
+Implement `CliServer` ‚Äî an `McpServer` backed by a spawned subprocess that speaks MCP JSON-RPC over its stdio.
+
+In `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/server/cli.rs`:
+- Constructed from `{ cli: Vec<String>, env: Option<Map>, cwd: Option<PathBuf> }` (the CLI `ServerSource` variant).
+- On construction/connect: spawn the subprocess, perform the MCP `initialize` handshake over stdin/stdout, send `tools/list`, cache the response as `Vec<ToolMetadata>`. Subscribe to `notifications/tools/list_changed` to refresh the cache.
+- `invoke(caller, tool, input)` ‚Üí send a JSON-RPC `tools/call` (tool name + arguments map, unchanged) over stdin, await the matching response on stdout, return the result `Value`. Errors map to platform `Error`.
+- Manage subprocess lifecycle: kill on drop/unregister; restart-on-crash policy (at minimum, surface a crash and fail subsequent calls cleanly ‚Äî full auto-restart can be minimal).
+- Use rmcp's client-side stdio transport if it fits; otherwise a thin JSON-RPC framing over `tokio::process::Child` pipes.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `CliServer` exists, implements `McpServer`, spawns the subprocess and completes the MCP handshake.
+- [ ] `tools()` reflects the subprocess's `tools/list`; `invoke` round-trips a `tools/call` over stdio.
+- [ ] The subprocess is killed when the `CliServer` is dropped/unregistered.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test in `swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/`: use a real, tiny MCP stdio server as the subprocess ‚Äî the `files` tool exposed as a standalone stdio binary, or a minimal fixture MCP server built in the test crate. Register it as a `CliServer`, call a tool, assert the result.
+- [ ] Test that dropping the `CliServer` terminates the child process (assert the PID is gone).
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-plugin` ‚Äî all green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write the subprocess round-trip test against a real stdio MCP server first, then implement.
+
+## Depends on
+McpServer trait + ToolMetadata.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -16,10 +16,10 @@
 - On construction/connect: spawn the subprocess, perform the MCP `initialize` handshake over stdin/stdout, send `tools/list`, cache the response as `Vec<ToolMetadata>`. Subscribe to `notifications/tools/list_changed` to refresh the cache.
 - `invoke(caller, tool, input)` ‚Üí send a JSON-RPC `tools/call` (tool name + arguments map, unchanged) over stdin, await the matching response on stdout, return the result `Value`. Errors map to platform `Error`.
 - Manage subprocess lifecycle: kill on drop/unregister; restart-on-crash policy (at minimum, surface a crash and fail subsequent calls cleanly ‚Äî full auto-restart can be minimal).
-- Use rmcp's client-side stdio transport if it fits; otherwise a thin JSON-RPC framing over `tokio::process::Child` pipes.
+- **Build on the rmcp client SDK** ‚Äî use rmcp's client-side stdio/child-process transport as the foundation; hand-roll JSON-RPC framing only for gaps rmcp does not cover. Stay consistent with the rmcp-everywhere principle.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `CliServer` exists, implements `McpServer`, spawns the subprocess and completes the MCP handshake.
+- [ ] `CliServer` exists, implements `McpServer`, spawns the subprocess and completes the MCP handshake via the rmcp client.
 - [ ] `tools()` reflects the subprocess's `tools/list`; `invoke` round-trips a `tools/call` over stdio.
 - [ ] The subprocess is killed when the `CliServer` is dropped/unregistered.
